title: Eriastrum diffusum
intro: Eriastrum diffusum, commonly known as the fairy lantern, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Eriastrum diffusum include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Eriastrum diffusum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Eriastrum diffusum is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Eriastrum diffusum is commonly known as the diffuse eriastrum or diffuse fairy lantern. As a member of the Polemoniaceae family, it is classified as a type of polemonium or "Jacob's ladder". The specific classification of Eriastrum diffusum is Eriastrum diffusum (A. Gray) Greene.
